_comment: Curated demo catalog for /mine/earn video activity. All entries are TED or TED-Ed talks, which TED publishes under CC-BY-NC-ND — explicitly allowing redistribution for non-commercial / educational use (https://www.ted.com/about/our-organization/our-policies-terms/ted-talks-usage-policy). The fetcher (scripts/fetch-earn-videos.sh) resolves each title to a YouTube ID via 'yt-dlp ytsearch1:', downloads the MP4, probes the real duration, uploads to Vercel Blob, and writes the resolved entries into app/lib/video-catalog.generated.json. Picking by title rather than hardcoding IDs avoids me inventing wrong IDs.

entries.title: Do schools kill creativity?
entries.speaker: Sir Ken Robinson
entries.search_query: Sir Ken Robinson Do schools kill creativity TED Talk
entries.blurb: The most-watched TED talk ever. Ken Robinson makes the case that schools educate creativity out of kids — and why that matters.

entries.title: The power of believing that you can improve
entries.speaker: Carol Dweck
entries.search_query: Carol Dweck power of believing that you can improve TED
entries.blurb: The Stanford psychologist who coined 'growth mindset' explains why praising effort beats praising talent.

entries.title: Inside the mind of a master procrastinator
entries.speaker: Tim Urban
entries.search_query: Tim Urban Inside the mind of a master procrastinator TED
entries.blurb: The Wait But Why writer maps what's actually going on when you can't start.

entries.title: What adults can learn from kids
entries.speaker: Adora Svitak
entries.search_query: Adora Svitak What adults can learn from kids TED
entries.blurb: A twelve-year-old writer arguing — convincingly — that grown-ups should think more like children.

entries.title: The best stats you've ever seen
entries.speaker: Hans Rosling
entries.search_query: Hans Rosling The best stats you've ever seen TED
entries.blurb: The late Swedish doctor who turned bubble charts into a sport. Watching him is a masterclass in seeing data.

entries.title: The power of introverts
entries.speaker: Susan Cain
entries.search_query: Susan Cain The power of introverts TED
entries.blurb: Why the world needs the quiet ones — and what schools and offices get wrong about teamwork.

entries.title: 3 rules to spark learning
entries.speaker: Ramsey Musallam
entries.search_query: Ramsey Musallam 3 rules to spark learning TED
entries.blurb: A chemistry teacher on what really makes a kid want to learn — and the heart surgery that taught him.

entries.title: All it takes is 10 mindful minutes
entries.speaker: Andy Puddicombe
entries.search_query: Andy Puddicombe All it takes is 10 mindful minutes TED
entries.blurb: The Headspace co-founder on why doing nothing — properly — is the most useful thing you can do.

entries.title: The power of vulnerability
entries.speaker: Brené Brown
entries.search_query: Brené Brown The power of vulnerability TEDxHouston
entries.blurb: A storyteller-researcher on shame, courage, and why letting yourself be seen is the harder choice.

entries.title: The surprising habits of original thinkers
entries.speaker: Adam Grant
entries.search_query: Adam Grant The surprising habits of original thinkers TED
entries.blurb: The Wharton psychologist on why the best ideas come from people who procrastinate and doubt themselves.

entries.title: How to make stress your friend
entries.speaker: Kelly McGonigal
entries.search_query: Kelly McGonigal How to make stress your friend TED
entries.blurb: What if stress isn't your enemy? A health psychologist on the study that changed her mind.

entries.title: The puzzle of motivation
entries.speaker: Daniel Pink
entries.search_query: Daniel Pink The puzzle of motivation TED
entries.blurb: Why carrot-and-stick incentives stop working — and what does for creative work.

entries.title: How sugar affects the brain
entries.speaker: Nicole Avena
entries.search_query: TED-Ed How sugar affects the brain Nicole Avena
entries.blurb: A neuroscientist explains the dopamine loop behind that 'just one more cookie' feeling.

entries.title: The benefits of a good night's sleep
entries.speaker: Shai Marcu
entries.search_query: TED-Ed The benefits of a good night's sleep Shai Marcu
entries.blurb: Why pulling an all-nighter actually hurts your memory — explained through the hippocampus.

entries.title: How does the immune system work?
entries.speaker: Emma Bryce
entries.search_query: TED-Ed How does the immune system actually work Emma Bryce
entries.blurb: B-cells, T-cells, and the immune memory that lets you only get chicken pox once.

entries.title: Why we love repetition in music
entries.speaker: Elizabeth Hellmuth Margulis
entries.search_query: TED-Ed Why we love repetition in music Elizabeth Hellmuth Margulis
entries.blurb: Why earworms stick, and what repetition does to how we hear a song.

entries.title: How smart are dolphins?
entries.speaker: Lori Marino
entries.search_query: TED-Ed How smart are dolphins Lori Marino
entries.blurb: Mirror tests, tool use, and what a dolphin's giant brain is for.

entries.title: The history of chocolate
entries.speaker: Deanna Pucciarelli
entries.search_query: TED-Ed The history of chocolate Deanna Pucciarelli
entries.blurb: From a bitter Aztec drink to the bar in your pocket — a 3,000-year story.

entries.title: How stress affects your brain
entries.speaker: Madhumita Murgia
entries.search_query: TED-Ed How stress affects your brain Madhumita Murgia
entries.blurb: Cortisol, the hippocampus, and what chronic stress actually rewires.

entries.title: How stress affects your body
entries.speaker: Sharon Horesh Bergquist
entries.search_query: TED-Ed How stress affects your body Sharon Horesh Bergquist
entries.blurb: Walking through one stressful day and what every organ is doing about it.
